description: Test vectors for J-PAKE Round 2: computation of A = (g^x1 + g^x3 + g^x4)^(x2 * s) and zero-knowledge proof with modified base point. Round 2 uses the sum of three public keys as the ZKP generator, not the curve generator.

protocol_overview.name: J-PAKE Round 2 (RFC 8236)
protocol_overview.prerequisite: Round 1 complete - both parties have g^x1, g^x2, g^x3, g^x4

round2_format.description: Round 2 exchanges A and B values with zero-knowledge proofs

A.description: A = (g^x1 + g^x3 + g^x4)^(x2 * s) (P-256 X coordinate)

zkpA.description: Zero-knowledge proof for x2*s with base (g^x1 + g^x3 + g^x4)

B.description: B = (g^x3 + g^x1 + g^x2)^(x4 * s) (P-256 X coordinate)

zkpB.description: Zero-knowledge proof for x4*s with base (g^x3 + g^x1 + g^x2)

password_scalar.description: s is the password converted to a scalar

round2_zkp_structure.name: Schnorr Zero-Knowledge Proof (Modified Base)
round2_zkp_structure.description: Proves knowledge of x2*s such that A = GA^(x2*s) where GA = g^x1 + g^x3 + g^x4. CRITICAL: The base point is NOT the curve generator G.

commitment.description: V = GA^v where v is random nonce (Schnorr commitment with modified base)

challenge.description: c = H(GA, A, V, party_id)[0:16] truncated hash

response.description: r = v - c*(x2*s) mod n (Schnorr response)

verification.note: Uses GA as base, not G; A is the public value being proven

test_vectors.name: Round 2 message structure validation
test_vectors.description: Verify Round 2 message has correct size and opcode

test_vectors.name: Round 2 ZKP structure validation
test_vectors.description: Verify Round 2 zero-knowledge proof has correct component sizes

test_vectors.name: Round 2 requires Round 1 values
test_vectors.description: Round 2 computation requires all four Round 1 public keys

test_vectors.name: Exponent scalar multiplication
test_vectors.note: Scalar multiplication must be mod curve order n

test_cases.note: x2 * s mod n should produce 32-byte scalar

test_vectors.name: Round 2 determinism with fixed keys
test_vectors.description: Given same Round 1 keys and password, Round 2 A should be deterministic

assertions.when: same gx1, gx3, gx4, x2, and password

test_vectors.name: Round 2 varies with password
test_vectors.description: Different passwords produce different A values

test_vectors.name: Round 2 state transition
test_vectors.description: State machine transitions on Round 1 completion

test_vectors.name: Round 2 serialization roundtrip
test_vectors.description: Serialize and parse Round 2 data correctly

test_vectors.name: Round 2 rejects wrong state
test_vectors.description: Attempting Round 2 from wrong state throws error

xdrip_compatibility.description: Compatibility notes with xDrip libkeks Round 2 implementation

getRound3Packet_mapping.note: xDrip names this getRound3Packet, but it's J-PAKE Round 2 conceptually
getRound3Packet_mapping.computation: val x134 = x1.add(x3).add(x4).normalize(); val A = x134.multiply(x2s).normalize();

wire_format.note: Full 64-byte EC points in xDrip wire format

wire_format_examples.name: Round 2 TX (client to sensor)
wire_format_examples.description: Client sends A with ZK proof

wire_format_examples.name: Round 2 RX (sensor to client)
wire_format_examples.description: Sensor responds with B and ZK proof

key_insight.title: Modified ZKP Base Point
key_insight.description: Unlike Round 1 where ZKP proves knowledge of x such that Y = G^x (using curve generator G), Round 2 ZKP proves knowledge of x2*s such that A = GA^(x2*s) where GA is the SUM of three points from Round 1. This is critical for protocol correctness.
key_insight.security_implication: The password is embedded in the exponent (x2*s), but the ZKP never reveals either x2 or s individually.
